title: A solution for detection of a viral rna sequence
abstract: The invention relates to a solution for detection of a viral RNA sequence the solution comprising a double stranded (duplex) initiation helix, at least one signal system, and at least two probes A and B having hairpin structures. The invention further relates to a kit comprising said solution, and the use of said solution for e.g. detection of viral RNA in a sample.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A solution for colorimetric detection of a viral RNA sequence, wherein the solution comprises: a double stranded (duplex) initiation helix, wherein the initiation helix comprises two strands, a template strand having an aâ, bâ, and câ domain and a recognition strand having an a, b, c, and d domain, wherein the template strand is fully complimentary to the recognition strand, and wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence; at least one signal system formed from a first group and a second group; at least two probes A and B, wherein probe A is a hairpin structure M1 and probe B is a hairpin structure M2, wherein M1 is a nucleotide sequence having an a, b, c, bâ, and aâ domain and M2 is a nucleotide sequence having a, b, câ, bâ, aâ, and c domain; wherein the first group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A and the second group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A or probe B; wherein the a domain is complimentary to the aâ domain, the b domain is complimentary to the bâ domain, and the c domain is complimentary to the câ domain; and wherein the recognition strand is complimentary to the viral RNA sequence to be colorimetric detected. 2. The solution according to claim 1 , wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence composed partially of Locked Nucleic Acid (LNA), Intercalating Nucleic Acids (INA), or combinations hereof, and the toehold sequence is at least 9 nucleotides. 3. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the second group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A. 4. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the solution does not comprise enzymes, i.e. the solution is an enzyme-free solution. 5. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one signal system formed from the first group and the second group is a quencher-fluorophore signalling system, a Forster resonance energy transfer (FRET) signalling system, a gold- nanoparticle signalling system, or a florescent nanoparticle signalling system. 6. The solution according to claim 6, wherein the at least one signal system formed from the first group and the second group is a quencher-fluorophore signalling system or a fluorophore-quencher signalling system. 7. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the toehold sequence is between 10 and 20 nucleotides, such as between 14 and 16 nucleotides. 8. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the a and aâ domain sequences are both between 4 and 10 nucleotides, such as between 5 and 9 nucleotides, such as between 6 and 8 nucleotides. 9. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the b and bâ domain sequences are both between 5 and 11 nucleotides, such as between 6 and 10 nucleotides, such as between 7 and 9 nucleotides. 10. The solution according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the c and câ domain sequences are both between 10 and 20 nucleotides, such as between 12 and 18 nucleotides, such as between 14 and 16 nucleotides. 11. A kit for colorimetric detection of a viral RNA sequence, wherein the kit comprises a solution and a sample collector for collecting sample from a patient, wherein the solution comprises: a double stranded (duplex) initiation helix, wherein the initiation helix comprises two strands, a template strand having an aâ, bâ, and câ domain and a recognition strand having an a, b, c, and d domain, wherein the template strand is fully complimentary to the recognition strand, and wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence; at least one signal system formed from a first group and a second group; at least two probes A and B, wherein probe A is a hairpin structure M1 and probe B is a hairpin structure M2, wherein M1 is a nucleotide sequence having an a, b, c, 

 bâ, and aâ domain and M2 is a nucleotide sequence having a, b, câ, bâ, aâ, and c domain; wherein the first group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A and the second group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A or probe B; wherein the a domain is complimentary to the aâ domain, the b domain is complimentary to the bâ domain, and the c domain is complimentary to the câ domain; and wherein the recognition strand is complimentary to the viral RNA sequence to be colorimetric detected. The kit according to claim 11 , wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence composed partially of Locked Nucleic Acid (LNA), Intercalating Nucleic Acids (INA), or combinations hereof, and the toehold sequence is at least 9 nucleotides. The kit according to any of claims 11-12, wherein the kit further comprises lysis and extraction solutions for lysing and extracting the total viral RNA from the sample, such as the nasopharyngeal swab sample. Use of a solution for detecting a viral RNA sequence in a sample, wherein the solution comprises: a double stranded (duplex) initiation helix, wherein the initiation helix comprises two strands, a template strand having an aâ, bâ, and câ domain and a recognition strand having an a, b, c, and d domain, wherein the template strand is fully complimentary to the recognition strand, and wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence; at least one signal system formed from a first group and a second group; at least two probes A and B, wherein probe A is a hairpin structure M1 and probe B is a hairpin structure M2, wherein M1 is a nucleotide sequence having an a, b, c, bâ, and aâ domain and M2 is a nucleotide sequence having a, b, câ, bâ, aâ, and c domain; 

 wherein the first group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A and the second group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A or probe B; wherein the a domain is complimentary to the aâ domain, the b domain is complimentary to the bâ domain, and the c domain is complimentary to the câ domain; and wherein the recognition strand is complimentary to the viral RNA sequence to be colorimetric detected. 15. The use of a solution according to claim 14, wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence composed partially of Locked Nucleic Acid (LNA), Intercalating Nucleic Acids (INA), or combinations hereof, and the toehold sequence is at least 9 nucleotides. 16. A solution for use in detection of a viral RNA sequence in a sample, such as a sample from a patient, wherein the solution comprises: a double stranded (duplex) initiation helix, wherein the initiation helix comprises two strands, a template strand having an aâ, bâ, and câ domain and a recognition strand having an a, b, c, and d domain, wherein the template strand is fully complimentary to the recognition strand, and wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence; at least one signal system formed from a first group and a second group; at least two probes A and B, wherein probe A is a hairpin structure M1 and probe B is a hairpin structure M2, wherein M1 is a nucleotide sequence having an a, b, c, bâ, and aâ domain and M2 is a nucleotide sequence having a, b, câ, bâ, aâ, and c domain; wherein the first group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A and the second group of the at least one signal system is covalently attached to probe A or probe B; wherein the a domain is complimentary to the aâ domain, the b domain is complimentary to the bâ domain, and the c domain is complimentary to the câ domain; and wherein the recognition strand is complimentary to the viral RNA sequence to be colorimetric detected. 

The solution for use in detection of a viral RNA sequence in a sample according to claim 16, wherein the recognition strand d domain is a toehold sequence composed partially of Locked Nucleic Acid (LNA), Intercalating Nucleic Acids (INA), or combinations hereof, and the toehold sequence is at least 9 nucleotides.
